evidence: The present is a past-haunted celebration.
The party is loud, the music is live, and the drinks flow freely.
But even in this joy, a past love surfaces, a regret for moments not captured, photos not taken, kisses not given.
Time moves on; friends have children, and priorities shift from superficial things to what truly matters.
The revelry is a way to live fully, acknowledging the past without letting it stop the dance.
You wish you had taken more photos, given more kisses, held onto those moments.
